channel.title: NPR Topics: Science
channel.description: The latest health and science news. Updates on medicine, healthy living, nutrition, drugs, diet, and advances in science and technology. Subscribe to the Health & Science podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Scientists probe how pigeons use magnetism to navigate
item.description: Homing pigeons rely on a variety of signals to navigate, including magnetism. But it hasn't been clear how they detect magnetic cues. Researchers propose the answer may be found in the birds' livers.
item.content:encoded: Homing pigeons rely on a variety of signals to navigate, including magnetism. But it hasn't been clear how they detect magnetic cues. Researchers propose the answer may be found in the birds' livers.
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: A science powerhouse bets on genetic therapy to beat brain disorders
item.description: After spending two decades studying how the brain works, a team of scientists prepares to focus on gene therapy treatments for brain diseases, including Alzheimer's and Parkinson's.
item.content:encoded: After spending two decades studying how the brain works, a team of scientists prepares to focus on gene therapy treatments for brain diseases, including Alzheimer's and Parkinson's.
item.dc:creator: Jon Hamilton

item.title: President Trump seeks control of science funding
item.description: The White House Office of Management and Budget is moving to take more control of billions of dollars in federal grants. Critics say the proposed change would jeopardize the integrity of U.S. science.
item.content:encoded:
The White House Office of Management and Budget is moving to take more control of billions of dollars in federal grants. Critics say the proposed change would jeopardize the integrity of U.S. science.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle

item.title: We finally know enough about how the brain breaks to focus on fixing it, experts say
item.description: The Allen Institute in Seattle says scientists have now learned enough about how the brain works to start fixing it when it breaks.
item.content:encoded:
The Allen Institute in Seattle says scientists have now learned enough about how the brain works to start fixing it when it breaks.

(Image credit: Erik Dinnel)
item.dc:creator: Jon Hamilton

item.title: The Trump administration aims to pull science funding under tighter political control
item.description: A Trump administration rule could shift how science funding works in the U.S. The administration says its an effort to deter waste, but science advocates say it will compromise scientific integrity.
item.content:encoded: A Trump administration rule could shift how science funding works in the U.S. The administration says its an effort to deter waste, but science advocates say it will compromise scientific integrity.
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle

item.title: 5 ways to reduce everyday exposure to 'forever chemicals'
item.description: Mara Hoplamazian has spent years reporting on 'forever chemicals,' or PFAS. Here's what they've learned about what may help limit everyday exposure to the contaminant.
item.content:encoded:
Mara Hoplamazian has spent years reporting on 'forever chemicals,' or PFAS. Here's what they've learned about what may help limit everyday exposure to the contaminant.

(Image credit: show999/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Mara Hoplamazian

item.title: New research helps explain how human brains are able decode speech so quickly
item.description: Scientists are learning how the brain extracts discrete words from a continuous stream of sounds.
item.content:encoded: Scientists are learning how the brain extracts discrete words from a continuous stream of sounds.
item.dc:creator: Jon Hamilton

item.title: The potential consequences of cutting funds for behavioral science
item.description: The Trump administration has proposed federal funding cuts to a little corner of research called social, behavioral and economic science.
item.content:encoded: The Trump administration has proposed federal funding cuts to a little corner of research called social, behavioral and economic science.
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle

item.title: An entomologist ordered a mezcal, and then got curious
item.description: An entomologist researched the worms used in mescal to better understand the relationship between the creatures and specific agave plants - and the danger if harvesting increases.
item.content:encoded: An entomologist researched the worms used in mescal to better understand the relationship between the creatures and specific agave plants - and the danger if harvesting increases.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel J. Sánchez

item.title: Experimental pill promises new hope for deadly pancreatic cancer
item.description: A novel pill helped people with advanced pancreatic cancer live longer, researchers reported Sunday, raising hopes of long-needed better treatments for one of the deadliest types of cancer.
item.content:encoded:
A novel pill helped people with advanced pancreatic cancer live longer, researchers reported Sunday, raising hopes of long-needed better treatments for one of the deadliest types of cancer.

(Image credit: Min Yu/AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
